KATHMANDU, Sept 19: Nepal’s premier folk rock band is set to travel to South Korea to perform at the Olympic Hall of Seoul Olympic Park.

Nepathya will perform at the renowned Seoul Olympic Park also known as Olpark is an Olympic Park built to host the 1988 Summer Olympics. In 2011, the Olympic hall was renovated by the Korean Government converting it into a concert hall. Musicians of international acclaim like Deep Purple, Judas Priest, Mariah Carey, Air Supply have performed there since.

This is to be Nepathya’s 5th appearance in the South Korean capital Seoul. “We came here for the first time in 2005 and since then Seoul has played regular host to us. Our last show was at the prestigious KBS Arena, and now we are performing at the Olympic Hall. We are glad to be raising the bar of Nepali shows there.” Said Kiran Krishna Shrestha of nepa~laya, the promoters of Nepathya.

“I am thankful to all our listeners living in Nepal and beyond for accepting our music with such affection’’, said Nepathya front man Amrit Gurung. “We are glad to be performing in Seoul for the fifth time during Nepathya’s 25 years of Music.” He added.
